expectations.text: [phase_contract][advisory] Case focus is explicitly covered: blind scenario drafting captures prompt handling, template save, report builder loading, and visible report title outcomes
expectations.evidence: The output explicitly covers prompt handling, template save, report builder loading, and visible report title outcomes (e.g., result.md lists all four and maps adjacent issues to each), but it does not explicitly cover “template save” as a drafting capability beyond naming it, and it does not cover “template save” + “report builder loading” + “visible report title outcomes” as outcomes in an actual Phase 4a draft artifact. It also lacks any concrete evidence of “template save” being captured in a produced scenario draft (execution_notes.md: “No actual runtime run directory artifacts... were provided”; result.md: “structure only; not claiming these were generated”).

expectations.text: [phase_contract][advisory] Output aligns with primary phase phase4a
expectations.evidence: result.md repeatedly anchors to Phase 4a scope and constraints: “This benchmark is phase4a…”, “Phase 4a contract explicitly forbids canonical top-layer categories… requires… subcategory → scenario → atomic actions → observable verification leaves”, and references Phase 4a entry/output/validation (“Entry: scripts/phase4a.sh”, “Output: drafts/qa_plan_phase4a_r<round>.md”, “--post validates”).
